title: Warm-up: inverted animal pyramid
description: Create an image of an inverted animal pyramid: an elephant on top of a horse on top of a dog on top of a cat on top of a mouse. The stack should be clearly readable, with each animal recognisable and roughly in its expected proportions relative to the others.

title: Pizzeria visual
description: Create a visual for a Pizzeria. It should show a scene associated with the Pizzeria. You can try anything — interior, storefront, dish, staff at work, branded photography. The goal is to obtain something useful that could realistically be used in a real setting (menu cover, social post, website hero, flyer).

title: Synthetic training data: camera trap of a rare serval
description: Create a realistic example of a camera trap image showing a rare serval. The image should be plausible enough to use as additional synthetic training data for a wildlife-monitoring detector: correct species morphology (long legs, oversized ears, spotted coat, short tail), nighttime or low-light grainy look typical of trail cameras, natural African savanna context, slight motion blur, infrared-leaning colour cast where appropriate.

title: Out-of-distribution example: wine glass
description: Generate an image of a wine glass full of red wine. It should be full to the brim.

title: Out-of-distribution: Anything you like
description: Try to come up with a prompt that the model fails to generate accurately because it is out-of-distribution.

title: Match the brand campaign: character family
description: Recreate the target image as closely as you can using text only. Pay close attention to the matryoshka-doll character lineup ordered by size, the 3D stylised character shapes (rounded, slightly oversized heads, simple expressive faces), the warm food-brand colour palette, what each character is holding, and the overall ad-photography feel against a neutral background.
